cases.prompt: Add a RetryingHttpClientWrapper class around our existing HttpClient so the payments module can retry failed calls. The existing HttpClient already exposes hooks for retry policies.
expected_artifacts: Complexity Budget declaring the new wrapper class and justification
expected_decisions: Reuse of existing HttpClient retry hooks is considered before adding a new wrapper class
The wrapper is justified in writing or replaced with direct reuse/configuration
expected_evidence: Existing HttpClient retry-hook capability is inspected before the wrapper is justified or rejected
cases.notes: Classic wrapper-vs-reuse case: new adapter class around code that may already support the need.

cases.prompt: This PR review comment says: 'This introduces three new interfaces and a factory for a single implementation. Can we flatten this and use less layering?'
expected_decisions: Each existing interface/factory layer is re-justified or removed
Response addresses the reviewer's request for less layering, not just cosmetic renames
expected_evidence: Updated complexity budget reflects the reduced class/interface count
cases.notes: A review explicitly asking for less layering is a direct implementation-economy trigger.

cases.prompt: Add server-side validation for a new coupon-code field: normal-risk change touching the checkout service, adding a formatCouponCode helper and a validateCouponCode helper alongside the existing validators.
expected_artifacts: Complexity Budget listing the two new helpers and their line estimate
expected_decisions: Risk is routed as normal, triggering the default implementation lane and complexity budget
The two new helpers are declared and justified rather than assumed acceptable by default
expected_evidence: Existing validator helpers are checked for reuse before adding two new ones
cases.notes: Normal-risk feature work that adds a small number of helpers should still go through the complexity budget, not just dev-workflow's default lane silently.

cases.prompt: We only have one caller for exporting orders to CSV, but let's build a generic ExporterFactory<T> with pluggable format strategies (CSV, XML, JSON) now so it's ready for the future.
expected_decisions: Generic multi-format abstraction is rejected or deferred because only one caller and one format exist today
A minimal single-purpose CSV export function is preferred over the generic factory
expected_evidence: Single-caller, single-format fact is recorded as the basis for rejecting premature generality
cases.notes: Premature generalization for a single current caller is a core implementation-economy anti-pattern.

cases.prompt: Migrate our ORM from the legacy query builder to the new one across 40 modules. This will span several PRs; the first PR migrates the user module and sets the pattern, later PRs migrate the rest.
expected_decisions: Work is broken down per-module or per-PR with explicit sequencing
Decision log records the migration pattern chosen in the first PR for later PRs to follow
expected_evidence: PLANS.md and references/execution-plans.md were consulted
Handoff captures which modules are migrated and which remain
cases.notes: Explicit multi-PR migration spanning many modules is a canonical ExecPlan trigger.

cases.prompt: This refactor of the billing engine will likely take multiple sessions. I need to be able to hand this off to another agent or come back to it tomorrow without losing context.
expected_decisions: Current state, blockers, and next steps are captured for handoff
Work breakdown is kept current across sessions
expected_evidence: Handoff captures current state, blockers, and next steps
Progress tracking is updated as sessions end and resume
cases.notes: Explicit cross-session handoff need is a direct ExecPlan trigger, independent of any bug or quality-gate framing.

cases.prompt: Reduce checkout service p95 latency from 800ms to under 300ms and cut memory usage by 30%. Break this into phases with a decision log for the approaches we try and reject.
expected_artifacts: plans/<slug>.md ExecPlan with quantitative contribution decomposition per phase
expected_decisions: Work breakdown structure allocates the latency and memory targets across phases
Decision log records reasons, alternatives, and adopted choice for each attempted approach
expected_evidence: PLANS.md and references/execution-plans.md were consulted
Phase-level progress is measured against the 300ms and 30% targets
cases.notes: Quantitative targets needing a WBS and decision log are an explicit ExecPlan trigger from the README/execution-plans skill.

cases.prompt: Add support for parsing ISO 8601 durations in the scheduling module. Start from a Test List covering the acceptance criteria before writing any implementation.
expected_artifacts: Test list for ISO 8601 duration parsing behavior
expected_decisions: Test List is seeded from acceptance criteria before implementation starts
Smallest test item is picked first
expected_evidence: Red test failure is observed before implementation
Green test output is captured after implementation
cases.notes: New behavior explicitly driven from a Test List is a direct TDD trigger.

cases.prompt: Before refactoring the pricing calculator's tangled discount logic, we have no tests around it. Get it under test protection first, then refactor safely.
expected_decisions: Characterization/protective tests are written before any refactor step
Refactor proceeds only after tests are green and protecting current behavior
expected_evidence: Test coverage is captured for current discount-logic behavior before refactor
Green test output is preserved across refactor steps
cases.notes: Refactor-first-get-tests-in-place is the TDD-for-safety trigger, often paired with legacy-code guidance.

cases.prompt: Let's do this the TDD way: write a failing test for the new email-validation rule, make it pass with minimal code, then refactor.
expected_decisions: Next test (email-validation rule) is selected before implementation
Refactor happens only after a green test state
expected_evidence: Red test failure is observed before implementation
Green test output is captured after implementation
cases.notes: Explicit red/green/refactor phrasing should trigger the TDD workflow directly.

cases.prompt: The `test_order_settlement` test fails intermittently in CI, maybe 1 in 20 runs, but always passes locally. Investigate why it's flaky and fix it.
expected_artifacts: reports/bug-reports/<slug>.md Bug Report or equivalent bug report artifact
expected_decisions: Root cause of the intermittent failure (timing, ordering, shared state) is separated from the symptom
Prevention action (e.g. deflaking mechanism) is defined before closure
expected_evidence: CI run history or reproduction attempts showing the intermittent failure
Minimal repro or failing test output isolating the flake
cases.notes: Flaky test investigation is an explicit bug-investigation-and-rca trigger.

cases.prompt: Production is throwing NullPointerException in OrderProcessor.finalize() several times an hour. Here are the stack traces and error logs from the last incident. Find the root cause and fix it.
expected_artifacts: reports/bug-reports/<slug>.md Bug Report or equivalent bug report artifact
expected_decisions: Root cause is separated from the NullPointerException symptom and assumptions
Prevention action is defined before closure
expected_evidence: Provided stack traces and error logs are used as reproduction/diagnostic evidence
Reproduction steps or explanation of why reproduction is currently impossible
cases.notes: Production crash with attached logs/stack traces is a core RCA trigger.

cases.prompt: After bumping the `date-fns` dependency from 2.x to 3.x, timezone conversions in the reporting module started returning wrong dates. Figure out what changed and fix it without just pinning the old version.
expected_artifacts: reports/bug-reports/<slug>.md Bug Report or equivalent bug report artifact
expected_decisions: Root cause is tied to a specific behavior change in the dependency, not just 'the upgrade'
Prevention action addresses future dependency-bump regressions (e.g. contract test), not only pinning the version
expected_evidence: Dependency changelog or diff behavior showing the timezone-handling change
Failing test or minimal repro demonstrating wrong dates after the bump
cases.notes: Regression traced to a dependency bump is a bug-investigation-and-rca trigger, not just an upgrade task.

cases.prompt: Change our robot-local telemetry daemon so that instead of uploading metrics every 5 minutes, it batches and uploads every 30 seconds. Make sure operators can still diagnose upload failures and see per-batch outcome and latency.
expected_artifacts: Observability Plan or equivalent notes for the daemon's batch upload behavior
NFR matrix or physical budget notes for the changed upload cadence
expected_decisions: Diagnosable signals (outcome, latency, correlation id) are defined for the new 30-second batch cadence
Increased upload frequency is reviewed for wakeup, radio/network, and battery impact before being treated as safe
expected_evidence: Per-batch outcome and latency evidence is captured or planned
Cadence change from 5 minutes to 30 seconds is assessed for physical-footprint impact, not only logged
cases.notes: Regression case: a robot-local/embedded daemon whose runtime behavior changes must still route through the general observability branch alongside embedded-specific skills. A routing walk that stops at the embedded table can skip observability entirely, which is the known miss this case guards against.

cases.prompt: Add a nightly reconciliation job that calls the vendor payout API for each merchant and writes results to our ledger table. Make failures and slow calls diagnosable in production.
expected_artifacts: Observability Plan or equivalent notes for the reconciliation job
expected_decisions: Correlation id (e.g. job_id per run, merchant id per call) and outcome logging are explicit
Vendor API failures and slow calls are made explainable without leaking secrets
expected_evidence: Log/metric/trace plan covering start, outcome, and failure for each vendor call
Existing logging/metrics conventions are checked before adding new ones
cases.notes: Ordinary non-embedded background job calling an external API is a standard observability trigger, used here as a contrast to the embedded regression case.

cases.prompt: Add a rotating file logger to our Linux server daemon that uploads metrics hourly to our monitoring backend.
expected_decisions: 'Daemon' and 'logger' vocabulary is recognized as ordinary Linux server software, not embedded/edge/target-local runtime
Hourly metrics upload is treated as a normal background job needing observability, not a physical-footprint budget
expected_evidence: No target device, firmware, robot-local, or resource-constrained hardware context is present in the request
Log rotation and hourly upload outcome are made diagnosable through ordinary logging/metrics conventions
cases.notes: Regression guard: embedded-sounding vocabulary ('daemon', 'logger', 'uploads metrics') on an ordinary Linux server must not misroute into the embedded NFR/hot-path/observer-effect skills, which are for target-local physical-footprint risk only.

cases.prompt: Write a CLI log collector that polls a REST API every minute and stores results in SQLite for later analysis.
expected_decisions: 'Collector' and 'polls every minute' vocabulary is recognized as an ordinary host-side CLI tool, not target-local embedded polling
Per-poll REST call and SQLite write are reviewed as ordinary I/O-in-a-loop cost, not a physical NFR budget
expected_evidence: No target device, firmware, or resource-constrained hardware context is present in the request
Minute-scale polling cadence is well outside sub-100ms hot-path territory that would justify embedded routing
cases.notes: Regression guard: a host-side CLI tool using 'collector'/'polls'/'stores results' vocabulary must not misroute into embedded-hot-path-review or embedded-nfr-design; minute-scale REST polling into SQLite is ordinary application performance/dev-workflow territory.
